channel.title: Services & Support on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science
channel.description: Recent content in Services & Support on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science

item.title: RCE Documentation
item.description:
RCE Documentation

Extensive documentation is available on the Research Computing Environment (RCE) site.

item.title: RCE Account Renewal
item.description:
RCE Account Renewal

As required by Harvard security policy, all RCE accounts have a renewal date. Renewal dates are selected based on the estimated end times of users’ projects. As a general rule accounts are up for renewal at least once every year. When you see an account renewal message, please respond to it. If you fail to respond, your account will be disabled.

item.title: RCE Accounts
item.description:
RCE Accounts

When you request an RCE account, username will be the University NetID. RCE account passwords must conform to the following restrictions:     At least eight characters in length     Not based on any common dictionary word     No spaces     Contains at least two numbers     Contains at least two special (nonalphanumeric) characters Please contact us for more information.

item.title: KVM Access to Servers
item.description:
KVM Access to Servers

Remotely via a web browser – This is only working with older version of the Java JRE (<=1. 6. 0. 10 on Windows and <=1. 6. 0. 7 on Linux) as of 2009-05-14. Open a web browser and navigate to https://dsview. hmdc. harvard. edu/. Log in using your LDAP username and password.

item.title: Monitoring
item.description:
Monitoring

We check the status of our services continuously. If a problem is detected at any time, we are notified immediately. If you experience a problem with any of our services, please check http://mon. hmdc. harvard. edu before contacting support. If your problem is listed there, our staff was notified and we are working on a resolution.

item.title: Physical Security
item.description:
Physical Security

Our data center is designed to maximize the availability of physical resources. The power infrastructure is served by a large-scale generator, uninterruptible power supplies, and filtered city power. The data center has redundant air conditioning units, and is equipped with a preaction, gas-based fire suppression system.

item.title: Research Cluster Hosting
item.description:
Research Cluster Hosting

We provide acces to our existing Cluster Computing infrastructure with an RCE account. If you have recurring or continual needs for greater resources than are available through our standard service offerings, we might be able to work with you to purchase additional equipment to supplement our public resources. We set up and manage the resources on your behalf.

item.title: Server Hosting
item.description:
Server Hosting

For basic research our RCE accounts include access to resources suitable for most purposes. If you have recurring or continual needs for greater resources than are available through our standard service offerings, we might be able to pursue another solution where additional resources are allocated to your research.

item.title: Remote Administration
item.description:
Remote Administration

For physical servers that we host, we offer administrative access without the need for physical contact through remote graphical consoles, serial consoles, and power management tools. Using the remote administration service in combination with our Monitoring service reduces drastically the time of most server outages.

item.title: Web Privacy Statement
item.description:
Web Privacy Statement

The following discloses our information gathering and dissemination practices for this web site (HMDC Support). Information Gathering Our web server software generates logfiles of the IP addresses of computers that access this web site and of what files they access. These web server logs are retained on a temporary basis and then deleted completely from our systems.

item.title: HMDC Usage Policies
item.description:
HMDC Usage Policies

Our products and services are governed by the policies described in this section. Members of our user community must agree to abide by the relevant policies before making use of any HMDC-hosted service or application.

item.title: Confidential Information Policy Summary
item.description:
Confidential Information Policy Summary

Confidential Information Policy Summary It is the responsibility of IQSS staff and affiliates to be aware of University rules governing the collection, storage, transport, use, and disposal of confidential information, and to follow these rules. Below is a brief summary of these policies, with links to the full details.

item.title: Backup Policies
item.description:
Backup Policies

Except where otherwise stated, HMDC-provided storage services are backed up by default. The schedule and retention policy varies depending on the aplication. Generally, back ups are retained for 3 months. The highest chance of success for recovering a file occurs if it exists on disk for 24 hours and HMDC is notified as soon as possible when corruption or deletion occurs.

item.title: Hardware Policies
item.description:
Hardware Policies

Hardware policies and procedures are designed to provide optimal service to all users, both staff and faculty, in a cost effective manner. Only machines owned or purchased by the University will be supported. We recommend that departments and centers cycle their systems after four years.

item.title: Publication and Use of Personal Information Policy
item.description:
Publication and Use of Personal Information Policy

Harvard policy governs the publication and the use of identifiable information about students, staff, and faculty, and is defined at the following location:    http://security. harvard.

item.title: General Usage Policies
item.description:
General Usage Policies

Information stored on a computer system or sent electronically over a network is the property of the individual who created it. Systems administrators, however, may gain access to users’ data or programs when it is necessary to maintain or prevent damage to systems or to ensure compliance with other University rules. We train our systems administrators with care.

item.title: Web Technologies
item.description:
Web Technologies

We recognize that the web is an increasingly important communication medium for the departments and research centers at CGIS. HUIT currently offers a variety of web-based services that include websites, mailing lists, and event management systems. Each of these systems has proven valuable for members of the CGIS community. We support and assit with these technologies and others managed by HUIT.

item.title: Supported Technologies
item.description:
Supported Technologies

We rely extensively upon open standards products for interoperability, stability, and flexibility. Because we are committed to supporting a wide range of areas of academic endeavor, we avoid the constraints of proprietary solutions whenever feasible. Wherever applicable our infrastructure is built upon commodity hardware from major vendors.

item.title: Research Technology Resources
item.description: Research Technology Resources

item.title: Backup & Recovery
item.description:
Backup & Recovery

Keeping a good backup routine can spare you from unpleasant and sometimes costly computer problems. A computer’s hard drive (the part that stores your data) is one of the least reliable parts in your computer, which makes it extremely important to keep a backup of your important files. While the Unversity highly recommends backups be completed, they are not required.

item.title: Recommended Handheld Devices
item.description:
Recommended Handheld Devices

The devices listed here currently are recommended. Apple iPhone and iPad running a supported version of iOS/iPadOS Android phones and tablets running a supported version of Android OS Windows tablets running Windows 11

item.title: Hardware Purchasing & Recommendations
item.description:
Hardware Purchasing & Recommendations

Hardware Purchasing & Recommendations - OverviewWe are happy to assist affiliates with all steps of the hardware purchasing and ordering process. Types of hardware that we support include (but are not limited to) printers, desktop and laptop computers, handheld devices, and printers.If you would like assistance with purchasing hardware of any type, please contact us.

item.title: Hardware Support
item.description:
Hardware Support

We are proud to support a variety of hardware, including desktop computers, laptops, Harvard VOIP phones, printers, scanners, and a variety of smart phones and tablets. If you experience computing problems that you suspect are hardware related, please contact us. A support technician can assist you with hardware troubleshooting, in-warranty support requests, or hardware replacement purchasing if needed.

item.title: Hardware and Software Overview
item.description:
Hardware and Software Overview

We are happy to assist with a variety software and hardware issues, including installation, troubleshooting, and purchasing (through HCOM and FAS Enterprise Licensing). Support for hardware and software is available Monday through Friday, between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM (EST).

item.title: Recommended Printers
item.description:
Recommended Printers

General printer recommendations include HP Laserjet printers with networking and duplex options. For current printer recommendations please contact us. If you have an immediate need to print (color or b&w), please contact the IQSS Lab Helpdesk.

item.title: Recommended Desktops and Laptops
item.description:
Recommended Desktops and Laptops

We are happy to assist affiliates with all steps of the hardware purchasing and ordering process. Please note, tablet purchases are restricted by the Dean. Please contact your department administrator to seek an exemption. More info can be found here:https://science. fas. harvard.

item.title: Recommended Desktops and Laptop Computers
item.description:
Recommended Desktops and Laptop Computers

We recommend Dell laptops and desktops running Windows 11. We are contraced with Dell to have techs onsite for any hardware issues that may occur. For Macs, we recommend MacBook Pros or Airs for portable systems and Mac Minis or iMacs for desktops. All of our staff are Apple certified.

item.title: Desktop Computers
item.description:
Desktop Computers

We provide computer hardware support for CGIS affiliates, and are happy to assist departments and research centers in the procurement, management, and troubleshooting of desktop and laptop computers. We are trained to service PC and Macintosh computers, but only those owned by the University.

item.title: Safe Computing
item.description:
Safe Computing

Practicing Safe Computing One form of attack that is growing rapidly is the use of social engineering to infect your computer, or to gather sensitive information from you. These attacks can take the form of professional built, legitimate looking fake websites that prompt you to input sensitive data or even emails from people posing as your IT support that ask for your password.

item.title: Securing Remote Access
item.description:
Securing Remote Access

We recommend the use of the FAS VPN whenever connecting to Harvard from public networks. Set up instructions can be found here. HUIT Networking Operations offers customized firewall services for administrative and research purposes.

item.title: Restoring Files
item.description:
Restoring Files

To restore files that were backed up to an external hard drive, follow the protocols provided by your backup system (i.e. TimeMachine or Windows Backup)To restore files that were backed up to network drives or CrashplanProE, contact us for assistance.

item.title: Disposing Of Systems, Portable Storage, and Media
item.description:
Disposing Of Systems, Portable Storage, and Media

Whenever a system, storage device, or storage procedure is disposed of, assigned to another client, or repurposed, it must be cleaned to eliminate Harvard confidential information (HCI) data.

item.title: Transferring Files Securely
item.description:
Transferring Files Securely

Confidential information must be encrypted whenever it is sent over any network. Never send confidential information unencrypted in e-mail, even if the connection to the e-mail server is encrypted. High-risk confidential information may be sent only directly from one approved system to another.

item.title: Virus Protection FAQ
item.description:
Virus Protection FAQ

Below are answers to some of the most common questions users have about virus protection. If you have a question about virus protection that is not answered below, feel free to contact us. Why are my Crowd Strike Antivirus updates failing on my work computer? Start by checking your network connection.

item.title: Installing Virus Protection
item.description:
Installing Virus Protection

We provide Crowd Strike Antivirus to CGIS affiliates free of charge. For Harvard owned machines, Crowd Strike is pushed out automatically through Microsoft SCCM and Casper. For personal machines, please follow the instructions provided on this website below: https://security.harvard.edu/choosing-personal-anti-virus-software

item.title: Malware
item.description:
Malware

While Anti Virus software catches most malware, the occasional threat may sneak by. There are many options to scan your system. The most popular being MalwareBytes. This free application is generally regarded as safe and can be used to scan for and remove malware infections.  If you require further assistance or have questions, please contact us.

item.title: Backing Up Remotely
item.description:
Backing Up Remotely

Remotely backing up your data involves copying your important files to a location that is not attached directly to your computer. Generally, this means copying your data over a network to a location on a file server. The University provides many options including OneDrive, g. harvard, Sharepoint, departmental network shares and CrashPlanProE.

item.title: Scanning for HRCI
item.description:
Scanning for HRCI

All Harvard computers systems must be scanned annually for sensitive information. For CGIS affiliates, IQSS/HMDC is using a software package called Identity Finder to assist in confidential data scanning.  You will automaticly receive Identity Finder software through SCCM or Casper.To request a high-risk confidential information (HRCI) assessment of your department’s data, please contact the HUIT Help desk (617) 495-9000 or ithelp@harvard.edu.

item.title: Backing Up Locally
item.description:
Backing Up Locally

The term local backup refers to copying your important data to devices directly connected to your computer.

item.title: Self
item.description: Self-Service Resources

item.title: Zoom for Teaching, Workshops and Conferences
item.description:
Zoom for Teaching, Workshops and Conferences

There are a substantial number of guides that will teach you the mechanics of using Zoom for video conferencing, web conferencing, and webinars. What is missing, though, is a guide that answers the question, “now that I know how to use Zoom, how can I use it to teach, workshop, or hosting a large conference?” That is where this guide comes in.

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions
item.description:
Frequently Asked Questions

Open all sections

Close all sections

Can I eat in the lab?

expand_more

While we want you to keep hydrated, we ask that you do not display or consume food during your time in our labs. Please dispose of any food outside of our lab as we strive to keep our area free of mice, rats and other creatures that also love to consume wires.

item.title: Workspaces
item.description:
Workspaces

Enjoy our work spaces that provide a nice, office-like environment to help you get your work done. Each area comes equipped with monitors, keyboards and mice that enable you to connect your mobile device (laptops, tablets, and phones), providing you a larger visual workspace to work in.

item.title: Printing Information
item.description:
Printing Information

Printing InformationIQSS Computer Lab printers are available to all lab patrons. Any member of CrimsonPrintcan use our printers directly. Extra paper is stocked beneath the printers in the center of the Concourse Lab.

item.title: IQSS Computer Labs
item.description:
IQSS Computer Labs

Who can Access?

We host multiple state-of-the-art computing labs for faculty, visiting scholars, post-docs, students, and staff. The IQSS Computer Labs use University active directory accounts that must be activated for use in our labs. New patrons can easily “sign” up for lab access by filling out our access request form.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description:
Contact Us

Please use the following email address to submit your help request: researchconsulting_support@help. hmdc. harvard. edu Please provide the following information in the email: 1) Your full name. 2) Your role (e. g. , graduate student, professor, postdoc, etc. ). 3) Your affiliation (e. g. , Psychology dept. , Medical school, MIT Sloan, etc. ). 4) A short description of the issue you would like help with.

item.title: Research Output
item.description:
Research Output

2018 (n=32) IQSS Worthington, S. (in review) Hypodontia prevalence in permanent teeth: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Scientific Reports. Psych McAuliffe, K. , Blake, P. R. , & Warneken, F. (in review). Costly fairness in children is influenced by who is watching. Developmental Science. Psych Dillon, M. R. , Izard, V. , & Spelke, E. S. (in review).

item.title: Performance Metrics
item.description:
Performance Metrics

The following page offers key service and training metrics. Please, contact us if you need additional data on DSS performance.

Strategic Goals

chevron_right

Metrics

chevron_right

item.title: Tutorials
item.description:
Tutorials

LaTeX Applications: A Beginner’s Guide Created specifically for Gary King’s GOV 2020 class, this guide walks students through their recommended options for editing LaTeX code. Included is a comparison of Overleaf, VSCode, and Emacs, as well as recommendations for tools such as GitHub and Copilot.

item.title: Workshop Materials
item.description:
Workshop Materials

We offer a series of data analysis and programming workshop materials to members of the Harvard and MIT communities, as well as to the public. Each workshop is self-contained and can studied at your own pace.

item.title: Data Science Services
item.description:
Data Science Services

IQSS Data Science Services is pleased to assist researchers in the Harvard community working on publication research in overcoming obstacles at every stage of the research process. Advisory Services

Consulting

We provide short-term support (up to 3 hours per publication) in the form of advice.

item.title: Onboarding
item.description:
Onboarding

Pick up your Harvard ID:@Smith Campus Center (RM 807, Phone #617-495-7827)Claim your HarvardKey (Your HarvardKey enables access to Harvard applications and services, by using a single login name and password) and activate two-step verification. https://harvard. service-now. com/ithelp?id=kb_article&sys_id=518d43916fc1aec0a3a79b9eae3ee40fhttps://harvard. service-now.

item.title: Printing
item.description:
Printing

We install, troubleshoot, maintain, and recycle printers for all affiliates. We support many brands and models of printers and are happy to assist with your printing needs. Please note that wireless networking of printers is not supported on the Harvard network. This feature requires services that are best suited to home networks and cannot be used on campus.

item.title: Mapping (Connecting To) a Departmental Network Drive
item.description:
Mapping (Connecting To) a Departmental Network Drive

Windows 11:1) Click on the folder on the bottom bar2) Click This PC3) Click on the Computer tab4) Click Map Network Drive5) Enter the path to your department share. This may be on Dss10 or fas-deptsFor example, \fas-depts. ad. fas. harvard. edu\cgis\your_department_name or \dss10. ad. fas. harvard.

item.title: Tech Disposal
item.description:
Tech Disposal

Whenever a system, storage device, or storage procedure is disposed of, assigned to another client, or re-purposed, it must be cleaned to eliminate Harvard confidential information (HCI) data. Note: Deleting your files and or reformatting the hard drive may not be sufficient for this process.

item.title: Travel Consultation
item.description:
Travel Consultation

If you are traveling with a personal, Harvard-funded system (laptop, tablet, smartphone) or looking for a loaner provided by Harvard, please see the links below:https://www.huit.harvard.edu/international-travel-loaner-deviceshttps://www.globalsupport.harvard.edu/travel/pre-departure-support/form…https://www.globalsupport.harvard.edu/resources/travelersAnd if you have any further questions, please contact us.

item.title: Secure Systems
item.description:
Secure Systems

The Desktop Support group supports clients in setting up and maintaining systems that are secure for use with confidential information. Consistent with University policy, all University laptops and all desktop systems with access to file services must be configured securely.

item.title: Research Computing Support
item.description:
Research Computing Support

IQSS collaborates with FASRC to provide researchers with access to resources on the Cannon (data security level 2) and FASSE (data security level 3) compute clusters. To request an account and access to resources, please follow instructions here: https://docs.rc.fas.harvard.edu/kb/quickstart-guide/.

item.title: Our Services
item.description: Our Services

item.title: Confidential Data
item.description:
Confidential Data

It is your responsibility to follow Harvard’s policies on data security while working with our systems. Be aware that data that contains information about students or is related to Harvard’s business and operations can be classified as confidential and require special handling.

item.title: Connecting to the Harvard Network
item.description:
Connecting to the Harvard Network

All computers must be registered for use before they are able to connect to the Harvard network. The registration process differs based on the type of connection you are trying to access.

item.title: Virus Protection
item.description:
Virus Protection

We provide virus protection software and services, as well as virus removal assistance in cases of infection. There are many avenues that viruses and other malicious software (malware) can use to infect your computer. No computer attached to a network is ever completely secure, but there are steps you can take to make your computer’s security much stronger.

item.title: VPN Remote Access
item.description:
VPN Remote Access

To access your network space while off campus:Install and configure Okta app on your smart device if you haven’t done so already. Details can be found at https://key. harvard. eduInstall Harvard’s VPN Client: You can obtain this software from http://vpn. harvard. eduLog in with your Harvard username and Key and additional code from the Okta app.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description:
Contact Us

Thank you for contacting us. We work with our users continuously to develop and improve our services. We appreciate and encourage your comments, questions, and feedback. The most effective to reach us is by using this form. Your message is recorded automatically in our request tracking (RT) system, and is received by all the members of our user support team.

item.title: Zoom Resources
item.description:
Zoom Resources

Frequently Asked Questions

Open all sections

Close all sections

How to add and manage alternative hosts for Zoom meetings in my Canvas courses?

expand_more

https://harvard. service-now. com/ithelp?id=kb_article&sys_id=044c4a92dba7c85430ed1dca489619c1

How to request to record a meeting/Zoom class?

expand_more

The requestor will need to ask the host either verbally or in the chat.

item.title: Client Support Services
item.description:
Client Support Services

Our friendly, dedicated, and highly-skilled staff can provide support for all of your hardware and software needs. Get Help From Client Support Services

We’re here to help you with your technological needs including, but not limited to, computer, smartphone, tablet and printer issues.

item.title: Services We Offer
item.description:
Services We Offer

The IQSS Data Science Services (DSS) team supports researchers throughout the Harvard and MIT communities by helping them navigate challenges at every stage of the publication research process. Our team includes five consultants: Joshua Cetron, Noah Greifer, Jinjie Liu, Steven Worthington, and Dan Yuan.
